Title: EMS and rural emergency response
Question: Where are the longest EMS response gaps, and what long-term Cherokee-controlled options are being considered?
Ask: Request coverage maps, response-time data, funding agreements, ambulance locations, and written plans.

Title: Housing and home repair backlog
Question: How many citizens are waiting for housing, rental support, accessibility improvements, or repair help?
Ask: Request program criteria, waitlist totals, completed projects by district, and funding by program.

Title: Language access and fluent-speaker growth
Question: Which actions most increase Cherokee language use, fluent speakers, teachers, and access for citizens?
Ask: Track immersion capacity, classes, teacher pipeline, technology tools, and district-level access.

Title: Broadband and digital access
Question: Which communities still lack reliable internet and how are broadband funds being mapped?
Ask: Request project maps, grant reports, timelines, service availability, and public update schedule.

Title: Water
Question: Open
Source: Are water quality, air quality, pesticides, brownfields, and environmental risks mapped in a way citizens can understand?
LastUpdated: Request public GIS layers, testing data, permit comments, and district-level risk summaries.

Title: State tax transparency in Indian Country
Question: Which state taxes are being collected from Cherokee citizens or businesses inside the reservation, and what authority is claimed?
Ask: Track receipts, retailer responses, state guidance, tribal tax/license options, and written legal positions.

Title: Municipal agreements and cross-deputization
Question: Which city/county agreements affect Cherokee citizens, and are authority, training, fines, courts, and data-sharing transparent?
Ask: Request MOUs, cross-deputization terms, ticket procedures, fine handling, complaint processes, and renewal dates.

Title: At-large citizen access
Question: Do at-large citizens receive clear voting, services, records, and communication access?
Ask: Track ballot info, town halls, service eligibility, mailing updates, digital access, and official contact points.

Title: Education and tribal consultation
Question: Are schools and agencies making tribal consultation visible and useful to Cherokee citizens?
Ask: Request consultation notices, affected districts, signed affirmations, curriculum access, and compliance records.

Title: Public records / FOIA-GRA transparency
Question: Can citizens easily find what records were requested, what was produced, what was denied, and what still needs follow-up?
Ask: Track requests, response dates, exemptions claimed, documents received, and next follow-up date.

Title: Voter registration and district awareness
Question: How many citizens know whether they are registered, what district they are in, and who represents them?
Ask: Direct citizens to official election resources and track public deadlines without collecting private voter documents.
